meaning: v.打碎,粉碎n.打碎,粉碎adj.出色的
examples: They did not need to smash paint and canvas; they went beyond this to smash the whole continuity of logical thought.
We had to smash the door open.
Her new series is a smash hit.
The ore is pulverized in a crusher that can smash 300 tons of rock per hour.
He had to smash open a drawer that held the keys to a vault.
